subject: Public Health
title: Schistosomiasis: The Snail Disease
description: One-minute skill: Schistosomiasis: The Snail Disease.

hook.text: 240 million people are infected with parasitic worms that enter through the skin while wading in fresh water.

buildup.text: Schistosoma worms use freshwater snails as hosts—larvae released by snails burrow through human skin on contact.

discovery.text: Chronic infection damages the liver, intestines, and bladder—stunting growth and impairing cognition in children.

twist.text: A single dose of praziquantel cures infection—costing just 20 cents—but reinfection occurs without WASH improvement.

climax.text: Eliminating schistosomiasis requires both mass drug administration and snail habitat control through sanitation.

punchline.text: A 20-cent pill cures the disease. Only clean water prevents it.

quiz.question: How does schistosomiasis enter the human body?
options: Larvae burrow through skin during contact with fresh water
